questions.question: Which of the following best defines learning as psychologists use the term?

options.text: Any change in behavior, whether temporary or permanent
A relatively permanent change in behavior or knowledge resulting from experience
The acquisition of information through formal education
An automatic response to environmental stimuli that requires no prior experience

questions.explanation: Psychologists define learning as a relatively permanent change in behavior or knowledge that results from experience. This definition excludes temporary changes (like fatigue), changes due to biological maturation, and innate responses like reflexes.

questions.question: A baby automatically pulls its hand away when it touches something hot. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: A reflex is an unlearned, automatic motor response to a sensory stimulus. Pulling away from a hot surface is a reflexive action that does not require prior learning or experience, distinguishing it from the various types of learned behavior.
